summary: Russia launched a massive drone attack on Ukraine overnight, with nearly 200 drones targeting multiple regions including Kyiv, Odesa, and Zaporizhzhia. Ukrainian forces struck a major oil terminal in Saint Petersburg and targeted fuel infrastructure inside Russia, contributing to gasoline shortages in Moscow and St. Petersburg. Ukrainian officials report that US attention has shifted toward Iran, reducing diplomatic focus on Ukraine.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine with nearly 200 drones overnight, targeting Kyiv, Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, and other regions with casualties reported. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Ukrainian forces struck a major oil terminal in Saint Petersburg, a key petroleum transshipment hub in the Baltic region. (Source: @UkraineNow/81638)
A massive drone attack on Kyiv on June 2 killed 7 people and injured 90, with 51 hospitalized including two children. (Source: @UkraineNow/81636)
Russian gasoline shortages have spread to Moscow and St. Petersburg, with fuel rationing of 50–150 liters per customer at various stations. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548)
Enemy drones from Kursk Oblast entered Sumy Oblast, with groups moving toward Poltava, Kyiv Oblast, and Chernihiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/63781)
Zelensky stated Ukraine is no longer the primary US focus, with Iran now taking precedence in American attention. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30655)
Ukrainian Defense Minister Fedorov pledged 1,500 motorcycles for the Armed Forces by year-end, triple last year's figure. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30651)
A Ukrainian drone attack caused a weapons factory in Tula, Russia to catch fire and burn down on June 3, 2026. (Source: @eRadarrua/95218)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive overnight drone assault on Ukraine involving nearly 200 drones, killing 7 and injuring 90 in Kyiv alone. In retaliation, Ukrainian forces struck a major oil terminal in Saint Petersburg and have been systematically targeting Russian fuel infrastructure, contributing to severe gasoline rationing in Moscow and St. Petersburg. The conflict is intensifying across multiple fronts while Ukraine faces reduced US diplomatic attention as Washington shifts focus toward Iran.

points: Ukraine's Defense Minister Fedorov announced the Armed Forces will receive 1,500 motorcycles by the end of 2026, three times more than last year, to improve frontline mobility in difficult logistics and off-road conditions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30651)

Ukrainian forces conducted a successful strike on a major oil terminal in Saint Petersburg, one of Russia's key petroleum transshipment facilities in the Baltic region, coinciding with the opening of the St. Petersburg Economic Forum. (Source: @UkraineNow/81638)

Ukrainian drones targeted Russian fuel infrastructure including a weapons production facility in Tula that caught fire and burned down on June 3, 2026, with the attack attributed to sanctions enforcement. (Source: @eRadarrua/95218)

President Zelensky acknowledged that Ukraine has ceased to be the primary focus of US attention, with Iran now taking precedence, and expressed concern that the Ukrainian negotiating team has been waiting too long for engagement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30655)
In the Nikolaev region, women armed with shovels drove away military commissars, indicating domestic resistance to mobilization efforts. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30647)

The Kyiv metro continues to serve a dual purpose, transporting passengers during the day and functioning as a bomb shelter at night, reflecting the ongoing threat to the capital. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30642)

Russia launched a massive overnight drone attack on Ukraine using nearly 200 drones, targeting Kyiv, Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Sumy, and other regions with both reconnaissance and strike UAVs. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

Gasoline shortages have spread to Moscow and St. Petersburg, with rationing limits of 50–150 liters per customer; the ORTK network limits sales to 60 liters of gasoline and 100 liters of diesel per transaction. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548)

Russia has sharply increased gasoline imports from Belarus, with May sales on the St. Petersburg exchange rising 26-fold year-on-year to 23,640 tons, though this remains insufficient to offset domestic shortfalls. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548)
A weapons production facility in Tula, Russia caught fire and burned down on June 3, 2026, attributed to a Ukrainian drone attack under the hashtag #єСанкції. (Source: @eRadarrua/95218)
In Bryansk Oblast, a drone attack killed a crane operator from the company 'Bryanskenergo' and wounded another person. (Source: @tass_agency/378892)
The Russian Armed Forces detected and destroyed a truck carrying a Ukrainian UAV launcher in the SMO zone using a Geranium kamikaze drone. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30650)

Russian forces struck Kharkiv's Kholodnohirskyi and Osnianskyi districts, injuring six people including men aged 18, 66, 68 and a 58-year-old woman with blast injuries and glass wounds; two others suffered acute stress reactions. (Source: @UkraineNow/81641)
A reactive-type drone targeted Pisochyn in the Kharkiv area, while Dnipro remained unchanged. (Source: @eRadarrua/95225)
In Zaporizhzhia Oblast, one reconnaissance drone and two strike drone groups were detected, with strikes reported on Vilnyansk and Novomykolaivka. (Source: @eRadarrua/95237)
In Zaporizhzhia district, a strike hit an unspecified location, with civilian infrastructure affected. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Enemy drones from Kursk Oblast entered Sumy Oblast, with a new group of strike UAVs crossing the border. (Source: @kpszsu/63781)
A group of hostile drones from Sumy Oblast moved toward Poltava Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/63792)
Hostile drones were detected on the border of Chernihiv Oblast and Belarus heading south, with vectors toward Kyiv Oblast through southern Chernihiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/63765)
Drones in Sumy Oblast are moving toward Kyiv Oblast via southern Chernihiv Oblast, with additional vectors toward Esman, Konotop, and Ichnya. (Source: @kpszsu/63788)
Current drone concentrations include 3 strike groups in Sumy Oblast, 2 in Chernihiv Oblast (plus 1 reactive group), 1 in Zhytomyr Oblast, and 2 in Kyiv Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/95197)
A ballistic missile threat was issued for Iskander-M launches from Bryansk Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
Hostile drones from the Black Sea are moving along the border of Mykolaiv and Odesa oblasts on a northern course toward Odesa. (Source: @kpszsu/63784)
A drone was detected in the Black Sea waters near Chornomorsk. (Source: @kpszsu/63761)
In Odesa Oblast, a guided drone targeted Novoukrainka and a strike drone hit Dachne and Rozdilna. (Source: @eRadarrua/95245)
In Odesa Oblast, enemy forces attacked civilian infrastructure damaging a truck and a service building at a parking lot; two men were wounded, one hospitalized. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
A new drone from Zaporizhzhia was reported targeting Slavhorod. (Source: @eRadarrua/95235)
Russia launched nearly 200 drones in a single overnight attack across Ukraine, targeting multiple oblasts simultaneously with both strike and reconnaissance UAVs. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Multiple drone types were employed including reactive (rocket-powered) drones, with reactive-type UAVs detected in Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kharkiv areas. (Source: @kpszsu/63780)

Ukrainian air defenses were active across numerous regions with drone tracking reported in Zhytomyr, Kyiv, Sumy, Chernihiv, Odesa, Mykolaiv, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kharkiv oblasts. (Source: @eRadarrua/95197)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast was issued, indicating continued use of precision ballistic weapons. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
Two cruise missiles were reported heading toward Yurivka, with warnings also issued for Dnipro and Pavlohrad. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67471)

key_developments: Russia launched a massive overnight assault on Ukraine with nearly 200 drones targeting Kyiv, Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, and multiple other regions, killing 7 and injuring 90 in the capital alone. (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

Ukrainian forces struck a major oil terminal in Saint Petersburg, a critical petroleum transshipment hub in the Baltic region, on the same day the St. Petersburg Economic Forum opened. (Source: @UkraineNow/81636)

Gasoline rationing has been imposed in Moscow and St. Petersburg with limits of 50–150 liters per customer, driven by Ukrainian strikes on refineries producing over 30% of Russia's gasoline and 25% of its diesel. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23548)

Enemy drones from Kursk Oblast entered Sumy Oblast with groups moving toward Poltava, Kyiv Oblast, and Chernihiv Oblast, while Black Sea-launched drones targeted Odesa and Mykolaiv regions. (Source: @kpszsu/63781)

A weapons production facility in Tula, Russia burned down on June 3, 2026 following a Ukrainian drone attack, representing continued deep-strike capability against Russian military-industrial targets. (Source: @eRadarrua/95218)

President Zelensky stated that Iran has replaced Ukraine as the primary US foreign policy concern, with Ukraine now 'in the queue' behind Tehran, potentially affecting diplomatic and aid timelines. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30655)

Ukraine's Defense Minister committed to delivering 1,500 motorcycles to frontline units by year-end, tripling last year's supply to address critical mobility needs in challenging terrain and logistics conditions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30651)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is sharply escalating with Russia deploying nearly 200 drones in a single night across multiple vectors, indicating sustained mass-production of UAVs and a strategy of attrition against Ukrainian air defenses and civilian infrastructure. Ukraine's continued deep strikes against Russian energy infrastructure — including the Saint Petersburg oil terminal and Tula weapons factory — are having measurable strategic effects, as evidenced by gasoline rationing in Moscow and St. Petersburg. The combination of reduced US diplomatic attention and intensified Russian strikes suggests Ukraine faces a challenging period requiring sustained Western military support to maintain defensive and retaliatory capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Belarus has significantly increased gasoline exports to Russia, with May shipments to St. Petersburg rising 26-fold year-on-year to 23,640 tons, though this remains a fraction of Russian monthly consumption. Russia's Kirill Dmitriev discussed resuming business dialogue between Russia, Germany, and the US with a representative of the German Advisory Group, suggesting ongoing back-channel economic diplomacy despite the conflict. The St. Petersburg Economic Forum proceeded despite the oil terminal attack, with Putin scheduled to speak about economic successes.
sections.intelligence_notes: The geographic spread of drone attacks — from Sumy and Chernihiv in the north through Kyiv and Poltava to Odesa, Mykolaiv, and Zaporizhzhia in the south — demonstrates Russia's ability to sustain multi-axis UAV operations simultaneously. The use of reactive (rocket-powered) drones alongside conventional strike and reconnaissance UAVs indicates a diversified and evolving Russian drone arsenal. Ukrainian domestic tensions are evident as women in the Nikolaev region physically confronted military commissars, suggesting mobilization fatigue. The reported destruction of a Ukrainian UAV launcher truck by a Russian Geranium drone in the SMO zone indicates active counter-UAV operations by Russian forces.
